analyst_observations: Both versions achieve 100% pass rate on all assertions — the verify.sh addition does not degrade quality
Token usage is nearly identical (~20k with_skill vs ~19.7k old_skill, +1.9%) — verify.sh adds negligible overhead
Duration is nearly identical (~72s vs ~70s) — no meaningful performance difference
The quantitative metrics cannot distinguish these versions — the difference is structural, not measurable by assertions
Key qualitative difference: with_skill produces JSON evidence artifacts that persist on disk; old_skill produces only in-context text
with_skill separates automated (5) from manual (2) checks explicitly; old_skill presents all 7 as equivalent manual steps
The real value of verify.sh is NOT visible in simulated evals — it prevents agents from simulating output in production use, which these simulated evals cannot test
